releases.notes:
### Added

- Added command palette actions for saving the current sidebar search and reopening saved searches
  without returning to the sidebar controls.
- Added a missing-page workbench that scans unresolved wikilinks in the current scope, shows source
  pages and counts, suggests page types, and starts creation with the linked title filled in.
- Added a Shuffle control for finite Random Table decks and bags so the remaining pool can be
  randomized without refilling or changing the discard pile.

### Changed

- Updated the in-app guide and implementation plan with English and pt-BR notes for missing-page
  cleanup, saved search palette access, and deck/bag shuffling.

### Added

- Added finite Random Table draw modes so a table can work like a Deck or Bag, draw without
  replacement, track remaining and discarded entries, refill the pool, and return one discarded
  entry.
- Added saved sidebar searches for each vault, including query, page type, tag, and focused campaign
  filters with quick apply and delete controls.

### Changed

- Updated the in-app guide and implementation plan with English and pt-BR notes for deck/bag table
  draws and reusable sidebar search filters.

## Added

- Added gated local AI random-table imports for Pro users with Beta features enabled, including
  Ollama setup guidance, model downloads, review-before-create import flow, and pt-BR copy.
- Added gated local AI monster imports for Tormenta20 and OSE screenshots, reusing the editable
  OCR review flow before creating or filling monster pages.

## Changed

- Changed the default local vision model to `minicpm-v`, which is the model that completed the
  integrated Moonvault import test successfully.
- Animated the AI import waiting state so it is clearer when Moonvault is still reading the image.
- Aligned monster sidebars, summary cards, and encounter combat labels with each monster's rules
  system so Tormenta20 uses `ND`, `Def`, and `PV`, OSE uses `HD`, `AC`, `HP`, and saves, and
  Shadowdark/D&D monsters avoid fields from other games.

## Fixed

- Kept the AI import review modal open on local model failures and added a timeout message when
  Ollama takes too long to answer.
- Preserved more native Tormenta20 and OSE monster fields from imported statblocks, including
  Tormenta20 perception, attributes, skills, equipment, and OSE saving throws.

### Changed

- Reworked the page header so navigation, document controls, and session workflow actions read as
  separate areas without turning the Session header into a wall of buttons.

### Added

- Added random-table OCR import for table screenshots, including a review step that creates one
  rollable Random Table page per detected table and keeps the source image attached.
- Added regression coverage for all 15 supplied table screenshots, including multi-table d100
  pages, compact lookup tables, and Tormenta20 PT-BR tables.

### Fixed

- Improved OCR scoring for table-heavy screenshots so headers like `Tabela`, `Resultado`,
  `Rolagem`, `Dano`, `Perícias`, `Divindade`, `Classe`, and `Raça` help select better OCR output.

### Fixed

- Filtered saved templates and Rules pages by the focused campaign's rules system so other-system
  prep stays out of the way.
- Improved Template sidebar actions with compact icon buttons for Browse and New.
- Fixed Live preview bold rendering when multiple `**bold**` spans appear in the same list item
  near wikilinks.
- Filled the pinned-page star button when a page is already pinned.
- Fixed Tormenta20 monster OCR for Gigante Bicéfalo-style statblocks.

### Fixed

- Fixed Tormenta20 monster OCR imports when the monster name and a noisy `ND` badge are read on
  separate lines, as in the Gigante Bicéfalo statblock.

### Fixed

- Fixed Tormenta20 monster OCR imports when red statblock labels are read with close OCR mistakes,
  including `IND (0 À` for `ND 10` and labels like `Deresa`, `Pontos oe Vio`, and `Corpo a Corro`.
- Split same-line Tormenta20 `Equipamento` and `Tesouro` OCR text into their correct monster fields.

### Fixed

- Fixed Live preview image paste so copied screenshots and image files become Moonvault image
  attachments and insert normal Markdown image embeds, matching the manual insert flow.

### Added

- Expanded explicit-opt-in PostHog observability for backups, updates, Template Studio, Map Room,
  live session capture, command palette usage, Pro feature interest, and export failures while
  keeping vault text, names, paths, images, and local folders out of analytics events.
- Added PostHog dashboard, schema, funnel, and cohort follow-up notes for turning the new
  privacy-safe events into useful product health views.

### Changed

- Updated the analytics wrapper to keep safe counts and booleans typed for PostHog dashboards,
  bound numeric values, use the current PostHog SDK defaults snapshot, and keep feature-flag checks
  on a short timeout.

### Added

- Added a first-vault-open analytics consent prompt with Yes, Not right now, Remind me later, and
  No, never ask again choices.

### Fixed

- Fixed the home-screen update notice so it installs through Moonvault's native updater instead of
  opening the release page, while still backing up the vault first when one is open.
- Fixed page and template navigation so choosing another sidebar or in-page link closes Settings
  instead of leaving the Settings panel open.
- Restyled template rows in the sidebar to match the normal page list instead of appearing as
  oversized bordered buttons.
- Tightened analytics tracking so `campaign_opened` fires only when a Campaign page is opened, not
  when another page silently updates the active campaign context.
- Fixed analytics startup so event `license_tier` is captured after Moonvault loads the local Free
  or Pro license state.
- Switched analytics transport to the official `posthog-js` SDK so `$pageview` and `$pageleave`
  events are emitted for PostHog Web Analytics health while custom event properties stay allowlisted.

### Added

- Added privacy-safe PostHog analytics behind an explicit Settings opt-in, with development builds
  disabled unless analytics is enabled through environment configuration.
- Added a local analytics wrapper with property allowlisting, bounded offline queueing, retry
  behavior, and no direct PostHog calls from UI components.
- Added first product events for app launches, campaign/page workflows, search, calendars, exports,
  sessions, and license activation outcomes using only safe metadata such as page type, RPG system,
  license tier, app version, platform, feature name, and error code.
- Added analytics documentation covering environment variables, consent, event names, safe
  properties, and vault data Moonvault intentionally does not collect.

### Added

- Added quick-roll sections to Shadowdark and D&D 5e starter Item and Ability templates, giving new
  system packs ready-to-use `/dr` prompts without adding extra setup.
- Added editable OCR review text for OSE and Tormenta20 monster screenshot imports, with a reparse
  action so GMs can correct recognition mistakes before creating or filling a monster page.
- Added a Vault Health Check repair for invalid world/campaign ownership frontmatter, removing dead
  ownership links while leaving Markdown prose untouched.
- Added a compact campaign prep summary to the prep checklist, showing session, encounter, targeted
  task, and linked-page counts with a direct jump to the next session.

### Added

- Added a Template Studio browser for searching saved templates by page type, folder, description,
  and body text before creating or editing from a reusable template.

### Changed

- Expanded Encounter Library cards with status filtering, richer party/monster counts, and clearer
  session-link messaging when remixing a prepared encounter.
- Expanded Generator Workbench results with kept-result counts and per-result drop controls, so GMs
  can keep strong rolls, remove weak ones, and save only the combined result they want.

### Added

- Added a Vault Health Check repair for stale relationship frontmatter references, removing dead
  relationship targets while leaving Markdown prose untouched.
- Added Shadowdark and D&D 5e starter templates for Item and Ability pages in new vaults, alongside
  the existing Tormenta20 and OSE packs.

### Changed

- Grouped Template Studio sidebar entries by page type and existing template subfolder path so
  larger template sets are easier to scan without changing vault-local Markdown template files.

### Fixed

- Kept wikilinks, dice rollers, random table rollers, and summary embeds inert inside inline code
  spans so examples stay readable in Preview and Live editor rendering.
- Rendered ordered Markdown lists, including mixed nested ordered/unordered lists, through the same
  preview pipeline that Live editor serialization already uses.
- Let OSE and Tormenta20 monster screenshot imports detect multiple OCR statblocks and choose the
  monster to import from the review modal instead of stopping at a crop-only error.

### Added

- Added previous and next Session navigation by campaign date in the Session header, sidebar, and
  command palette.
- Added month-title actions on fantasy calendars so GMs can create month-level Lore, Notes, or
  Events with the fantasy date already filled in.

### Fixed

- Fixed the signed native updater feed path so Moonvault can fetch valid update metadata before
  backing up the vault and installing an available patch.
- Mirrored the updater feed at the legacy `/releases/latest.json` path so Moonvault 1.60.0 and
  1.60.1 installs can still self-update.

### Fixed

- Fixed nested Markdown bullet lists in Live preview so switching from Markdown mode back to Live
  mode keeps indented subitems nested instead of flattening them into the surrounding session notes.

### Added

- Added a Settings and command palette **Back up vault** action that creates a timestamped zip of
  the complete vault folder beside the vault in `Moonvault Backups`.
- Added native updater wiring so Moonvault backs up the open vault before installing an available
  signed app update and restarting.
- Added release workflow support for Tauri updater signing secrets and a mirrored `latest.json`
  updater feed with bundle signatures.

### Changed

- Updated English and pt-BR guide copy to point GMs at the built-in backup workflow before imports,
  prep sprints, and app updates.
- Updated the Moonvault Pro Settings copy for the new Moonvault Premium purchase, claim-page, and
  lost-key recovery flow while keeping offline `MV1...` license activation.
- Expanded the release test battery with backup, updater, and updater-feed checks.

### Fixed

- Fixed Live editor paste so copied Moonvault formatting and wikilinks survive when moving content
  between pages.
- Fixed Live preview image rendering so sidebar/frontmatter edits do not turn image Markdown back
  into visible `![...](...)` text.
- Fixed nested bullet list editing so Tab/Shift+Tab and empty nested items keep Markdown and Live
  editor sublists properly indented.
- Fixed selecting text and typing `[[` so it creates a wikilink instead of replacing the selected
  text in Markdown and Live editor modes.

### Added

- Added a Copy summary action for generated dungeon and hex crawl summaries so GMs can paste
  revealed map notes outside the current page before inserting them as Markdown.
- Added a confirmation step before resetting a generated map so accidental clicks do not wipe the
  revealed dungeon rooms or hex crawl state.
- Expanded the in-app Maps guide in English and pt-BR with the experimental dungeon/hex generation
  workflow, summary copying, summary insertion, linked pages, and safer resets.

### Changed

- Localized the Map Pin editor in English and pt-BR, including linked-map hints, player-visible pin
  copy, placeholders, and fallback pin names.
- Localized generated-map inspector detail labels so pt-BR no longer sees English detail, danger,
  discovery, or resource labels inside Map Room.
- Moved Map Room label assembly out of the large render call into a memoized label bundle to make
  the map workspace easier to maintain.

### Added

- Added press-and-hold grid nudging so calibrated grids can move smoothly instead of one pixel per
  click.
- Added brush-style fog painting while dragging across the map, plus a cover-all fog command.

### Fixed

- Fixed the Map Room pin list layout after adding separate edit and remove actions.

### Changed

- Moved fog-of-war controls into the grid calibration toolbar so map controls stay in one place.
- Made hidden fog cells much more opaque while keeping them visible in player preview.

### Added

- Added first-pass fog-of-war painting for uploaded map grids, with GM-only hide/reveal cell
  controls saved in `mapFog` frontmatter.
- Added an edit action for existing map pins so GMs can convert pins between page links and notes
  and toggle player visibility after placing them.

### Fixed

- Fixed adding a map pin from Map Room so the pin modal no longer closes the map underneath it.

### Fixed

- Corrected hex grid positioning in Map Room so flat-top hexes stagger by column like common RPG
  hex-map templates.

### Added

- Added square and hex grid overlays to Map Room for uploaded map images.
- Added grid calibration controls for grid type, hex orientation, cell size, offset, visibility, and
  opacity.
- Saved uploaded-map grid calibration in readable `mapGrid` frontmatter without changing the
  original image.
- Added English and pt-BR copy for Map Room grid controls and the command palette Map Room action.

### Changed

- Expanded the in-app Moonvault guide from short operational notes into deeper workflow entries covering workspace orientation, vault safety, page graphs, Template Studio, imports/OCR, encounters, sessions, calendars, maps, and player-safe sharing.
- Added a guide search box, result count, no-results state, and table of contents so GMs can find advanced workflow help without scrolling through the whole guide.
- Replaced the fake screenshot placeholder frame with honest where-to-look callouts so guide panels no longer imply incorrect captured app screenshots.

### Added

- Added a local Moonvault user guide covering vault storage, backups, safe sync expectations,
  imports and OCR review, releases and updates, Pro licensing, and player-safe sharing.
- Added an **Open Moonvault guide** command palette action and a Settings help card so GMs can find
  the guide without opening the repository.
- Added English and pt-BR guide content plus tests that verify every supported language covers the
  required operational topics.

### Added

- Expanded English and pt-BR localization for page creation, campaign-required creation prompts,
  Template Studio create/rename/delete dialogs, session wrap-up copy, and session/exploration
  command palette actions.
- Added a pt-BR workflow smoke test covering creation, Template Studio, session wrap-up, and
  exploration command copy.

### Added

- Added Template Studio defaults so page-type templates can be marked as the automatic starting
  template for new pages of that type.
- Added default-template actions in the template header, template context menu, command palette,
  sidebar labels, and create-page template picker.

### Fixed

- Let Recent and Pinned sidebar sections be folded, dragged, reordered, and remembered like the
  page-type sections.

### Added

- Added drag-and-drop sidebar page-section ordering so GMs can move high-priority groups like PCs
  to the top and keep them there per vault.
- Added remembered collapsed state for page-section headers and the Templates header.

### Changed

- Page-type filters now follow the GM's saved sidebar section order.

### Added

- Added Template Studio management actions for creating, duplicating, renaming, and deleting
  vault-local page templates.
- Added a template context menu with duplicate, rename, and delete actions to match page sidebar
  context-menu behavior.

### Fixed

- Reworked the Templates sidebar create action so it no longer appears as an oversized floating
  square button.
- Tightened the top quick-action buttons so Calendar, Session, and Note labels fit cleanly inside
  their controls.

### Added

- Added a local `playerEmail` field to PC sheets so calendar invites can include the people playing
  each character without requiring Moonvault accounts.
- Added a Session invite review with date, time, duration, location, description, attendee emails,
  and clear warnings for PCs that still need a player email.
- Added Google Calendar handoff and portable `.ics` export from Session pages and the command
  palette, with English and Brazilian Portuguese copy.

### Changed

- Saved real-world invite details in Session frontmatter so GMs can revise and export the same
  schedule again without re-entering it.

### Fixed

- Rendered the Page actions menu in a document-level overlay so workspace rows can no longer paint
  over or clip its visibility, export, and delete controls.
- Kept the Page menu within the visible window with fixed positioning and bounded scrolling.

### Changed

- Grouped page navigation, save/pin status, editor mode, and page actions into a clearer toolbar.
- Moved Tools out of the vault/settings header and into the search/navigation utility cluster.
- Consolidated player visibility, PDF export, and page deletion under one localized Page menu.

### Fixed

- Fixed Page actions being painted beneath the properties and editor rows.
- Made Create, Filters, and Tools menus mutually exclusive so sidebar dropdowns no longer overlap.
- Added bounded scrolling and stronger stacking for long action menus.

### Changed

- Moved the Encounter Library and Generator Workbench into a compact Tools menu and restored the
  Calendar, Session, Note, and create-more actions to the top of the sidebar.
- Reorganized page type, tag, and campaign controls into one collapsible section that still shows
  the active navigation context while closed.
- Added English and Brazilian Portuguese copy for the reorganized sidebar controls.

### Fixed

- Added a minimize/expand control to the dice tray so the page list can use more sidebar space.
- Made the create-more menu scroll within the available window so every page type remains reachable.
- Made sidebar sizing resilient when update or export notices are visible.

### Added

- Added Vault Health Check for broken wikilinks, missing local assets, duplicate stable IDs, invalid
  world/campaign ownership, and stale relationship references.
- Added navigable diagnostics and an explicit safe repair for duplicate IDs that leaves Markdown
  prose untouched.
- Added complete English and Brazilian Portuguese copy for vault diagnostics and repair controls.

### Added

- Added a Generator Workbench that combines multiple existing random tables into one larger result.
- Added per-section result counts, individual rerolls, and reusable generator recipes stored on
  ordinary Markdown Note pages alongside the generated output.
- Added complete English and Brazilian Portuguese copy for the new generator workflow.

### Added

- Added an Encounter Library for finding and opening prepared battles from the command palette.
- Added Encounter Remix to copy a saved encounter, restore reusable participant state, preserve its
  notes, and optionally attach the new encounter to a Session without changing the original.
- Added complete English and Brazilian Portuguese copy for the new encounter workflow.

### Added

- Expanded Brazilian Portuguese coverage across template commands, import command palette actions,
  update notices, Settings copy, and template editor labels.
- Added localized Portuguese template titles and descriptions for newly created PT-BR vaults.
- Added a localization coverage test so supported languages keep matching translation keys.

### Changed

- Reworked Settings into grouped settings sessions for app behavior, writing defaults, vault
  creation defaults, Moonvault Pro, and campaign focus.

### Added

- Expanded the Brazilian Portuguese localization foundation across Settings, the welcome screen,
  sidebar navigation, page-type labels, and common command palette actions.
- Added Brazilian Portuguese starter Markdown templates and blank-page scaffolds for new vaults,
  worlds, campaigns, pages, calendars, encounters, maps, monsters, and random tables.

## Added

- Added smarter command palette page ranking with recency, aliases, exact-title matches, pinned pages, and current-campaign context so large vaults jump to the right page faster.
- Added vault default settings for new page visibility, new campaign system, and automatic campaign focus behavior.
- Moved Settings out of the modal and into a dedicated workspace page with section navigation for General, Editor, Vault defaults, Moonvault Pro, and Campaign focus.
- Added a Party history view that gathers recent sessions, encounters, discovered locations, faction ties, and open prep tasks connected to the party.
- Added the first localization foundation with a language and locale setting plus initial Settings strings for English and Brazilian Portuguese.

### Added

- Upgraded Party pages with a richer overview of member HP and AC, current locations, factions,
  active conditions, recent sessions, and related encounters.
- Added Settings options for the default editor mode and whether page properties open automatically.

### Added

- Added encounter-only HP, AC, and initiative bonuses for each combatant, plus a short field for
  other temporary effects without changing the source character or monster page.
- Added the current session summary to normal Session pages and Active Session Mode.

### Changed

- Session recap actions now say **Edit summary** after a recap has been saved.
- macOS releases now include a direct zipped app download, and the public release feed prefers it
  over the DMG so installing Moonvault does not mount extra desktop disk icons.

### Added

- Added optional monster nicknames in encounter sidebars so repeated monsters can be identified
  throughout turn tracking, initiative, and Active Session Mode.

### Added

- Added the campaign prep checklist directly to normal Session editor sidebars, with new tasks
  targeted to the open session automatically.
- Added a session-aware command palette action for quickly focusing the prep-task input without
  leaving the Session page.
- Bundled an offline WebAssembly OCR fallback with Portuguese and English language models for
  installed builds that do not have a native Tesseract executable.

### Changed

- Session prep views now show general campaign tasks and tasks for the open session while hiding
  tasks assigned to other sessions.
- Monster screenshot import still prefers native Tesseract when available, then automatically uses
  Moonvault's bundled OCR engine if the native command is unavailable.

### Fixed

- Preserved every line after Markdown list items when opening and saving content in the Live editor,
  including pasted statblocks that use `•` bullets and wrapped continuation lines.
- Preserved explicit line breaks when converting Live editor content back to Markdown.
- Made `/dr` create its clickable dice roller after a valid formula when the GM presses Space, Tab,
  or Enter in the Live editor.
- Moved the Markdown selection-format toolbar below and beside the selection so it no longer
  intercepts double-clicks.
- Opened newly created pages directly after command-palette creation, including Monster pages.

### Added

- Added regression coverage for mixed Markdown lists and nonstandard bullet characters.

### Added

- Added campaign prep checklists with completion tracking, optional target sessions, fantasy dates,
  and linked pages.
- Added prep checklist access from campaign pages, Active Session Mode, and the command palette.
- Added the full exploration tracker to Active Session Mode so GMs can advance dungeon turns,
  manage resources and conditions, update marching order, and record session awards without
  leaving the focused workspace.
- Added inline previous and next combatant controls for campaign encounters inside Active Session
  Mode.

### Changed

- Active Session Mode now includes dedicated Prep and Exploration sections alongside notes, party,
  encounters, random tables, and recent rolls.
- Encounter turn changes made from Active Session Mode update the normal Markdown-backed encounter
  page, including rounds and timed condition counters.

### Added

- Added page aliases through the `aliases` frontmatter property.
- Added alias-aware wikilink autocomplete. Choosing an alias inserts
  `[[Canonical Page Title|Selected Alias]]`, while links written directly with an alias still
  resolve to the correct page.
- Added year-only and month-only fantasy calendar links through `/date`, the Insert menu, and the
  command palette.
- Added a page-type chooser when creating a page from an unresolved `[[wikilink]]`.
- Added direct link target and display-text editing in the Live editor.

### Fixed

- Allowed existing Events to switch between exact-day, whole-month, and whole-year dates.
- Selected the current campaign automatically when creating an Event.
- Kept the Markdown selection-format toolbar out of the way while editing wikilinks.
- Made the Relationships panel available beneath the Markdown editor.
- Kept backlinks, page navigation, and player-safe exports aware of page aliases.

### Fixed

- Preserved the Live editor caret while the fantasy-date picker is open so Insert menu and `/date`
  selections are written back into the note at the intended position.
- Replaced the fantasy-date modal's month and day dropdowns with a calendar grid, month navigation,
  custom weekday labels, and a clearer selected-date summary.
- Reworked Active Session Mode into a focused section-based console so notes, party, encounters,
  and quick tools are easier to use during play.
- Made session notes directly editable and autosaving inside Active Session Mode.
- Replaced the oversized modal treatment for Active Session Mode and Living Timeline with dedicated
  focus views, clearer exits, compact timeline filters, and a more readable chronology.
- Corrected the focus-view header and sidebar action colors and removed a redundant Session console
  badge.

### Added

- Added the Finish session recap action to normal Session pages so GMs can complete the handoff
  without opening Active Session Mode.
- Added a Finish session recap prompt and a “Last time” card that carries the previous dated
  session's summary into the next Session Mode workspace.
- Added `@date[Month, Day, Year]` links that open the current world's fantasy calendar on the
  referenced day without creating a page.
- Added fantasy calendar date insertion to the editor Insert menu, command palette, and `/date`
  slash command.

### Changed

- Began decomposing the oversized application module by moving reusable fantasy-date UI and the
  Living Timeline workspace into focused components.
- Made Babel's development code-generation mode explicit so large-module transforms remain readable
  while the remaining application subsystems are extracted.
- Date insertion now opens the fantasy-date picker before changing the note, instead of immediately
  inserting a default date.
- Fantasy date chips now use a calendar icon and can be removed as one unit with Backspace or
  Delete in the Live editor.

### Added

- Added Pro Active Session Mode with session notes, party status, campaign encounters, random
  tables, and recent rolls in one focused table-running workspace.
- Added a Pro Living Timeline that orders world events around the campaign's current in-world date.
- Added timeline filters for past, current, and future events with direct navigation to the related
  Markdown pages.
- Added command palette and contextual page-header actions for both new workflows.

### Changed

- Kept session and timeline workspaces connected to the existing Markdown pages, relationships,
  encounter snapshots, random tables, roll history, and fantasy calendar instead of introducing
  separate dashboard-only data.

### Added

- Added recent pages to the sidebar and command palette so GMs can quickly return to material used
  during play.
- Added page type and tag filters alongside full-text sidebar search for faster navigation in larger
  vaults.
- Added standalone Map pages with their own map image, linked pins, and Markdown notes.
- Added a Map Room with separate GM and player-preview modes.
- Added player-visible map pins so landmarks can be previewed without revealing private GM notes.

### Changed

- Promoted maps into the normal create-page and sidebar workflows.
- Added an Open Map Room command palette action whenever the current page has a map.

### Added

- Added browser-style Back and Forward page navigation with toolbar buttons, command palette
  actions, and familiar keyboard shortcuts.

### Changed

- Refined the fantasy date modal so Year, Month, and Day are easier to identify and edit.

### Fixed

- Parsed fantasy date objects correctly in the native vault index so all dated Events appear
  reliably on their world calendar, including multiple Events on the same day.

### Added

- Added contextual Insert actions for birth, death, founding, destruction, and session boundary events.
- Added a focused date modal with clearly labeled Year, Month, and Day inputs.

### Changed

- Historical dates now create normal Event pages that link back to their PC, NPC, location, or session.
- Campaign current dates and Event dates now use the Insert menu instead of permanent Properties forms.

### Fixed

- Prevented date editing from collapsing the Properties panel.
- Kept legacy page date properties readable on calendars while removing their space-heavy editors.
- Kept Event dates visible in a highlighted page banner with a direct date-edit action.
- Saved Event and campaign dates immediately so navigation cannot discard them.
- Collected events consistently across world ids, titles, wikilinks, slugs, and world folders.
- Rendered every Event assigned to the same calendar day.

### Added

- Added a compact page Export menu with player visibility, full campaign packet export, and single-page PDF export in one place.
- Added command palette access for exporting the current player-visible page as a PDF handout.

### Changed

- Removed the separate vault header export button so page sharing and PDF actions stay together.
- Required pages to be marked Players before they can be exported individually, including native safety enforcement.

### Added

- Added a persistent PDF export button to the vault header so player packets are easy to find from anywhere.

### Fixed

- Kept GMs in their workspace after exporting a player packet and showed the saved result there instead of opening Settings.

### Added

- Added a recognizable Moonvault header, accent colors, campaign footer, and page numbers to player packet PDFs.
- Added simple row and column controls when a table is selected in the Live editor.

### Fixed

- Preserved bold, italic, bold italic, underline, inline code, quotes, and table structure in player packet PDFs.
- Rendered Markdown quotes as styled quote blocks in the Live editor.

### Added

- Added Pro player packet PDF export so GMs can share one familiar file without sending a vault or HTML folder.
- Added clear `GM only` and `Players` controls to page headers and Properties.
- Added an export safety review showing included player pages, excluded GM-only pages, and links to private notes that will be labeled as omitted.
- Added command palette access with a friendly Pro explanation for Free users.

### Fixed

- Enforced Pro licensing and player visibility again in the native export command so GM-only pages cannot be included through a UI bypass.
- Kept pages with missing or invalid visibility out of player packets by default.

### Fixed

- Hardened page navigation so Moonvault repairs any selected page/draft identity mismatch before autosave can write, preventing the Test of links body from appearing under or saving over City of Moon.
- Expanded regression coverage for the real TestVault link navigation failure across the selected-page repair path.

### Fixed

- Fixed Live editor wikilink navigation so the newly opened page always reloads its own body content instead of keeping the previous page's editable body.

### Added

- Added Pro-gated reusable page templates with template editing, dynamic template fields, and session template preselection.
- Added a Moonvault Pro link in Settings for lifetime Pro donation access.

### Changed

- Made license key generation use lowercase Makefile arguments, such as `name` and `email`, while keeping the old uppercase arguments working.
- Pointed the default 1Password license item references at the Moonvault vault.

### Fixed

- Closed editor Format, Insert, and template field menus when clicking outside them.

### Fixed

- Fixed macOS release packaging by ad-hoc signing the app bundle before publishing, preventing Apple Silicon downloads from being flagged as damaged.

### Added

- Added offline Moonvault Pro license activation with locally verified signed license keys.
- Added a 1Password-backed license key generation workflow for issuing customer licenses.

### Fixed

- Made the Pro badge readable on the dark sidebar.

### Changed

- Replaced the wide Markdown button strip with compact Format and Insert menus.

### Fixed

- Made Live Editor formatting actions apply visibly and consistently for code, headings, lists, quotes, and tables.
- Preserved quote formatting when Live Editor content is saved back to Markdown.

### Added

- Added more Markdown editing actions for code, headings, lists, quotes, and tables.
- Added a contextual formatting popover when selecting text in the Markdown editor.

### Fixed

- Kept manual update-check results inside Settings instead of showing the "up to date" message behind the modal.
- Made the campaign focus setting use the current page's campaign when one is available, so "Hide other campaigns" no longer looks mysteriously disabled while working inside a campaign.

### Changed

- Pointed the in-app update check at the new public release feed at `https://moonvaultrpg.com/releases.json`. Existing installs continue to update normally; future releases are mirrored to `moonvaultrpg.com` so downloads remain public after the source repository is moved to private.
